scope: custom: combat -- v2 weapon substrate (hands + head axes), Build-11
note: Implements the weapon_axes_v2 proposal bottom-up: hands (1H/2H) + head (point/cut_and_thrust/straight_cut/curved_cut/blunt) axes on the canonical reach/weight substrate; full reclassified roster (all weapons + tonfa short-blunt + war flail flexible-blunt); derived damage_class/armour(+point row)/str_mult/wield_min; v2 sigma-leverage modifiers (head bias by state, 2H bind amplification, 1H off-hand defence, curved draw-cut, flexible parry-bypass, tonfa defensive block). Self-test 7/7. TOP-DOWN VALIDATION (duel all-vs-all x armour + battlefield strikes-to-fell): blunt percussion (mace/poleaxe/war_flail) fell an engaged armoured foe in 3 strikes vs blades 5.5-6.3 (historically correct anti-armour); point (rapier) out-performs cut vs the armoured field (thrust gap-edge); sabre clusters with duelists (draw-cut+flow); war flail strong via parry-bypass; cut-and-thrust generalists mid (correct). ARTIFACT: bare mace tops the DUEL at 73.6% -- the SAME blunt-damage artifact as the war hammer (R10), now generalized: blunt percussion weapons are battlefield/forgiving weapons, NOT duel-balanced; their duel number is the wrong test (per R10 + the handling curve). NOT a damage nerf -- exclude blunt percussion from the duel-balance target, as with the war hammer. The one canon-touching piece is POINT_ARMOR_MOD (point-vs-armour row), flagged for ratification. Class A = canonical armour/STR/wield (combat_v30 §5 + W1 ratified). Class C = v2 sigma-leverage seeds (grounded; sim-tunable). Reuses M1/R1/R2. PROPOSAL -- Jordan-vetoable; no canon file edited. REFINED (iterations 1-4): added the handling skill-curve (handling_dsig, crossover proficiency 4 per m3 §8.2) + reach-control (reach_score: balanced heavy blade keeps reach, head-heavy blunt loses it) + the two-CONTEXT resolution (DUEL unarmoured = telling-hit finesse race, sigma-leverage governs, damage magnitude irrelevant; BATTLEFIELD armoured = attrition by damage, R10). Two grounded handling corrections from the research: longsword standard->DEMANDING (Liechtenauer is the canonical highly-technical art), war_flail demanding->FORGIVING (crude Hussite peasant equaliser, low ceiling; the parry-bypass is its quirk not skill). RESULT now historically COHERENT across all 3 contexts: skilled-duel rapier#1/longsword#5, mace#16 & war_flail#17 bottom; low-skill-duel forgiving weapons (messer/dagger/spear/staff/mace/war_flail) dominate & demanding fail; battlefield blunt fells in 3. Every weapon has its correct niche; the bare-mace artifact fully resolved by context+skill, no canonical damage nerf. Self-test 10/10.

entries.quoted_text: A point (light blade) finds armour gaps: +1 vs heavy where the cut goes to +0. Extends combat_v30 §5; PROPOSAL pending Jordan ratification (same status as W1).

entries.value: heavy x2 / light x1, blunt x1.5 / blade x1
entries.quoted_text: STR multiplier = weight-factor x type-factor (Heavy x2, Light x1, Blunt x1.5, Blade x1); heavy-blunt x3.
STR-min: base 1, +1 per Heavy, +1 per Long, +1 for the long-heavy-blunt war hammer (single_short=1 ... war hammer=4).
Point reaches on the approach and is weak once bound. Bottom-up: ratified reach/phase model (R9). Top-down: HEMA -- the thrust controls distance, the bound point is poor.
Curved-cut: draw-cut bonus vs unarmoured + flow/recovery tempo. Straight-cut: strong in the close/bind. Top-down: the seven-axes draw-cut (kris/sabre) + the percussive straight cut.
2H amplifies bind leverage (Stark/Schwach); 1H opens an off-hand buckler/dagger defence (I.33; Spanish daga); tonfa side-handle blocks. Bottom-up: ratified Strength-bind + Grip channels.
Handling skill-curve, crossover at proficiency 4: below 4 forgiving>standard>demanding, above 4 demanding>standard>forgiving. proficiency = relevant combat History.
Reach-control advantage on the approach; reach inverts once bound. reach_score refines R9's flat heavy-fraction: a balanced heavy blade keeps reach, a head-heavy blunt loses it.
